program_HK:
一部三角四維的音樂演義，拼出亞洲流行搖滾新版圖
亞藝無疆2026將由本地頂尖流行音樂班底率領，以草原的呼嘯、山海的節奏與都會的搖滾，三種維度的亞洲聲音揭幕。香港流行音樂金牌監製Carl叔叔夥拍本地薑「鼓神」恭碩良、炙手可熱結他手瞿俊僖、R.O.O.T主腦Jan Curious、擅長多種弦樂器的Danny Yau組成的本地樂隊將作東道主，策劃史無前例的四地合作。
「三角」陣容包括來自蒙古重金屬樂隊Uuhai，其獨特「蒙古馬頭琴X當代金屬音」的巨大氣場先征服歐洲樂迷，今首度駕臨香港；韓國新進搖滾樂隊KARDI帶來點燃戶外音樂節的爆發力，以「韓國玄琴X韓流女聲」刷新另類搖滾想像；而泰國indie-pop 組合KIKI的「夢幻節奏X輕快電音」，營造熱帶音樂氣氛。三角四地樂隊將遊走傳統與當代的界線，驚喜演繹流行音樂。
program_EN:
Three genres, four dimensions: Asian pop remapped as one
Asia+ 2026 opens with a bang. Three of Asia's most distinctive sounds — the steppes, the sea, and the city — collide on one stage, reinterpreting contemporary pop music in ways you won’t see coming. Hosting the charge is a stellar line-up of local pop artists: award-winning producer Carl Wong, drummer of Hong Kong’s biggest stars Jun Kung, sought-after guitarist Jason Kui, R.O.O.T vocalist Jan Curious and multi-instrumentalist Danny Yau, ready to spark moments that no setlist could predict.
Their guests come from three corners of Asia: Mongolian metal sensation Uuhai make their Hong Kong debut after conquering European stages, unleashing the horsehead fiddle against searing rock riffs; Korea's KARDI fuse female-vocal pop-rock with the resonance of the geomungo — a traditional six-string zither, delivering explosive power built for packed arenas. Thailand’s KIKI brings groovy indie-pop soaked in funky electronic beats, leaving a tropical haze. Blurring traditions and the new, all the bands get pop music a thrilling twist and reborn!

program_HK:
今年的壓軸戶外嘉年華「亞裔藝采+」以「禮意綻放」為主題，雲集超過20個「一帶一路」國家和地區的代表，以禮相聚，以藝傳情，共譜文化共鳴，綻放多元民族的獨特風采。
讓音樂與舞步化作祝福，每一段演出猶如一份精心呈上的無形禮物，承載不同文化的底蘊，讓觀眾感受不同文化中溫暖而真誠的情感。現場更設有文化攤位與工作坊，呈獻各國精緻工藝與特色小食，向觀眾送上來自不同民族的誠摯心意。
支持機構(File name: AECP Icons.pdf)
program_EN:
This year’s finale outdoor carnival, “Asian Ethnic Cultural Performances+,” is themed “Blessings in Full Bloom”. Bringing together representatives from over 20 Belt and Road countries and regions, it celebrates unity through ritual and emotion through art, creating cultural resonance and showcasing vibrant spirit of diverse ethnic groups.
Transforming music and dance into blessings, each performance unfolds like a thoughtfully presented intangible gift. Carrying the essence of different cultures, the performances allow audiences to experience the warm and sincere emotions deeply rooted in diverse traditions. The carnival also features cultural booths and workshops, presenting exquisite crafts and signature snacks from various countries, offering visitors heartfelt goodwill from different ethnic communities.
Supported by (File name: AECP Icons.pdf)

program_HK:
以銅訴說鼓的故事，一場觸動心靈的對話
馬來西亞頂尖敲擊樂團「手集團」攜手印尼峇里甘美蘭樂團呈獻《銅話鼓事》。作品將中國獅鼓、氣勢磅礴的馬來西亞二十四節令鼓，與飄逸靈性的甘美蘭音樂完美融合，在冷冽金屬與溫熱鼓皮的對比中，共譜一場跨越疆界的對話。
「甘美蘭」起源自千年前的爪哇和峇里的宮廷和寺院，被視為世界音樂的寶藏。演出中逾30組最具代表性的甘美蘭金屬片琴、銅鑼與鼓組，與獅鼓、節令鼓交織共振，融入傳統瑪蓉舞與歌曲，將經典昇華為當代「甘美蘭劇場」。銅音清透優雅，鼓皮之聲雄渾，在強烈反差中，探索峇里文化中「Taksu」點燃藝術熱情、直指人心的神聖能量。香港場更特別邀得香港兒童合唱團獻聲，詮釋以甘美蘭改編、家傳戶曉的中國民歌〈茉莉花〉。
拋開都市繁囂，讓原始樂音帶你尋回久違的生命力與心靈共鳴。
program_EN:
A dialogue stirring your soul and striking straight to your heart through percussion
Malaysia’s celebrated Hands Percussion brings a rare cross-cultural dialogue to Hong Kong, where the thunderous pulse of Chinese shigu meets the sonorous, sweetly flowing resonance of Balinese and Malay gamelan percussion, weaving the coolness of metal and the warmth of drum skin.
Widely regarded as one of the world’s musical treasures, gamelan was born in the royal courts and temples of Java and Bali over a thousand years ago. More than 30 sets of gamelan instruments, from shimmering bronze metallophones and gongs, to the Malaysian 24 Festive Drums rooted in Chinese drumming tradition, integrating with graceful Mak Yong dance and music, come together to interpret Gamelan into contemporary theatre. The striking contrast of bronze and animal skin evokes Taksu, an ancient Balinese belief in a sacred inner force that transforms art into something felt deep within the soul. For this special Hong Kong occasion, the Hong Kong Children’s Choir lends their voices to a gamelan reimagining of Jasmine Flower, one of the most beloved Chinese folk songs.
Leave behind the city’s hustle and bustle, and let the music help you rediscover the vitality you have long missed.

program_HK:
「一場高難度的奇觀，將西原傳說、世界級馬戲和當代舞共冶一爐，令人目眩神迷。」—美國《紐約時報》
越南版太陽馬戲團滿分製作　以高超雜技演繹千年高原文化
當代馬戲劇場《圓》帶領觀眾走進越南中部雲霧繚繞的高原深處，感受生機勃發的古老文化。
「Teh Dar」在西原格賀族語中意為「繞圈而行」，寓意生生不息。紅色舞台象徵西原的紅土地貌，雜技演員在千變萬化的竹藝結構上表演高空特技等驚險技藝。先祖的神話在密林間流傳，千年不滅的營火，點燃壯闊山巒之魂，一一呈現觀眾眼前。現場樂隊展現被列為聯合國非物質文化遺產的西原鑼鉦文化，以鑼鉦、水牛角、竹琴和象腳鼓等傳統樂器演奏。
《圓》神秘而充滿力量，歌頌經過時間洗鍊依然歷久常新的傳統文化。眉月製作素有「越南版太陽馬戲團」美譽，此作品於歐洲巡演後今首度登上香港舞台，不容錯過。
program_EN:
“A hypnotic, daring spectacle that fuses Central Highlands lore with world-class circus and contemporary dance.”—New York Times, USA
Vietnam’s Cirque du Soleil unveils the hidden beauty of ancestral highland
Deep in the mist-shrouded highlands of Vietnam, ancient traditions pulse with vitality. Teh Dar is a celebration of mountain culture and wild beauty in the form of contemporary acrobatic theatre.
In the K’Ho language, Teh Dar means “going in circles”, evoking the eternal cycle of life. On a stage reminiscent of the region’s distinctive red soil landscape, acrobatic performers conjure scenes of ancestral legend through daring aerial feats on evolving bamboo structures. Stories once sung deep within forests, echoed across soaring heights, and whispered around fires for generations now unfold vividly on stage. A live ensemble plays traditional instruments including gong, buffalo horn, T’rung and long drum, rooted in the Gong Culture of these highland communities, a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
Enigmatic, explosive and visceral, Teh Dar is a testament to humanity that continues to shine brightly across centuries. Widely acclaimed as Vietnam’s Cirque du Soleil, Lune Production now arrives in Hong Kong with The Dar after a successful European tour. A truly unmissable experience.

program_HK:
★★★★推介—英國《蘇格蘭人報》
音樂與書墨藝術的動人交會
在敘利亞裔演奏家神秘而豐富的中東與爵士旋律中，巴基斯坦裔視藝家以阿拉伯書法同鳴。
塔先．奇恩的書法融合阿拉伯、波斯和烏都語，三種語言出自同一書法源流，流麗的線條將中東古老新詮的音韻，化成看得見的律動。兩位阿迦汗大師音樂家費雷斯．查拉斯坦和巴素．拉祖，以中東卡農琴、亞美尼亞的杜克拉管，及西方的色士風和敲擊樂器，彈奏悠然和諧的韻律，奇恩的筆觸隨之翩然舞動，描繪出「希望」、「仁愛」、「正義」與「和平」。每晚完成的書畫都是獨一無二，並於演出翌日作公開展示。
《聲之墨境》去年在英國愛丁堡國際藝術節首次以舞台版演出，好評如潮。是次香港演出為亞洲首演，誠邀觀眾放下煩囂，享受「聲」與「墨」交織的出塵境界，洗滌心靈。
program_EN:
★★★★ Recommendation—The Scotsman, UK
Music and calligraphy become one in the vitality of arts
Pakistani-born visual artist Tazeen Qayyum improvises calligraphic drawing against a serene yet luminous tapestry of Middle Eastern and jazz-inspired tunes by Syrian-born Aga Khan Master Musicians Feras Charestan (qanun, a Middle Eastern plucked zither) and Basel Rajoub (saxophone, duclar and percussion).
Watch as graceful strokes of Arabic, Persian and Urdu script—three languages, one shared calligraphic tradition—spring to life, dancing to ancient melodies in contemporary interpretation. As the musicians switch between the Middle Eastern qanun, Armenian duclar, and the western jazz saxophone and percussion, Qayyum responds to the music through four words: amal (hope), karam (kindness), adl (justice) and silm (peace). Each night’s canvas, unique and unrepeatable, will be displayed the following day.
The stage edition received an overwhelming response at the UK’s Edinburgh International Festival 2025, now arriving in Hong Kong as its Asian premiere, inviting you to slow down and find inner peace in Canvas of Sound.
program_CN:
★★★★推介—英國《蘇格蘭人報》
音樂與書墨藝術的動人交會
在敘利亞裔演奏家神秘而豐富的中東與爵士旋律中，巴基斯坦裔視藝家以阿拉伯書法同鳴。
塔先．奇恩的書法融合阿拉伯、波斯和烏都語，三種語言出自同一書法源流，流麗的線條將中東古老新詮的音韻，化成看得見的律動。兩位阿迦汗大師音樂家費雷斯．查拉斯坦和巴素．拉祖，以中東卡農琴、亞美尼亞的杜克拉管，及西方的色士風和敲擊樂器，彈奏悠然和諧的韻律，奇恩的筆觸隨之翩然舞動，描繪出「希望」、「仁愛」、「正義」與「和平」。每晚完成的書畫都是獨一無二，並於演出翌日作公開展示。
《聲之墨境》去年在英國愛丁堡國際藝術節首次以舞台版演出，好評如潮。是次香港演出為亞洲首演，誠邀觀眾放下煩囂，享受「聲」與「墨」交織的出塵境界，洗滌心靈。

title_HK: 阿薩德二重奏結他音樂會
title_EN: Guitar Duo Recital by the Assad Brothers
title_CN: 阿薩德二重奏結他音樂會
program_HK:
「當今甚或是史上最好的結他二重奏……」—美國《華盛頓郵報》
古典結他傳奇告別巡演
來自巴西的阿薩德二重奏站在結他之巔逾半世紀，以高超技巧和非凡默契見稱。2026年適逢兄弟合作六十週年及中巴文化年，來港獻技以饗樂迷。
阿薩德二重奏的音樂糅合巴西民謠、拉丁、爵士和古典，獲多個拉丁格林美及格林美獎項肯定。他們曾合作的一流藝術家不計其數，曾與馬友友、甘祈頓和洛杉磯愛樂樂團等合作之外，阿根廷傳奇作曲家皮亞梭拉亦曾專門為他們譜曲。
今次演出作品涵蓋他們歷來最為樂迷津津樂道的金曲，包括皮亞梭拉的迷人探戈、巴西史上最偉大作曲家魏拉．羅伯斯筆下的本土風情和沙治奧．阿薩德的原創作品，更有在香港鮮有演出的巴西特色旋律。本地古箏演奏家萬幸將與兩位大師合演一首以花木蘭為靈感的全新樂曲，作為送給香港樂迷的特別禮物。

program_EN:
“...the best two-guitar team in existence, maybe even in history…”—Washington Post, USA
Classical guitar duo legend’s farewell tour
Sixty years at the top of the guitar world, Sérgio and Odair Assad bring their anniversary world tour to Hong Kong for an evening of unrivalled virtuosity and telepathic unity, where Brazilian folk, Latin, jazz and classical styles meet as one.
Winners of multiple Grammy and Latin Grammy Awards, the Brazilian masters have collaborated with the greatest names in classical music, including Yo-Yo Ma, Gidon Kremer and the Los Angeles Philharmonic. Piazzolla wrote works for them. The programme brings together their most iconic repertoire, from Piazzolla's mesmerising tango and Villa-Lobos’ Brazilian soul to Sérgio Assad’s own celebrated compositions, alongside Brazilian treasures rarely heard in Hong Kong. Local guzheng player Wan Xing joins in a special piece inspired by the legend of Mulan, in celebration of the 2026 China-Brazil Year of Culture and Tourism.

同樣身兼弦樂演奏家、巡迴音樂家和母親等多重身份的長期合作夥伴—瑞士音樂大獎得主大提琴家嘉貝蒂和格林美獎得主小提琴家科帕欽卡雅將舉行首個香港二重奏音樂會。她們將為樂迷帶來跨越多個時代的多首作品，早至巴赫、拉威爾、高大宜等人的經典作品，及多位當代作曲家包括科帕欽卡雅本人的創作。

title_HK: 第50樂季開幕音樂會︰千簧一宇
title_EN: The 50th Orchestral Season Opening Concert: Thousand Reeds, One Universe
title_CN: 第50樂季開幕音樂會︰千簧一宇
program_HK:
這是一場「可聽、可視、可參與」的文明體驗，讓「笙」從樂器升維至連接人類過去與未來的密碼。音樂會以香港為原點，讓觀眾聽見中國古老智慧在音樂中迴響——從晉北聲腔到正倉院唐笙遺制，從黔桂蘆笙到歐洲管風琴，完成簧屬樂器的完整譜系展陳。《寰宇同息》末段，指揮掌心面向觀眾席，引領未經擴聲的哼鳴以極弱滲入樂團織體——笙不再是「被演奏的對象」，而成為聯結所有呼吸的媒介。一簧一世界，千笙共宇和。
中華文化節2026節目之一
香港中樂團由香港特別行政區政府資助
program_EN:
A cultural exploration that combines listening, seeing and participation, Thousand Reeds, One Universe elevates the sheng from a musical instrument to a multidimensional code connecting humans with the future. Embarking from Hong Kong, this concert invites the audience to appreciate a musical tradition that reverberates with ancient Chinese wisdom. From Northern Shanxi tune-types to surviving Tang dynasty sheng artefacts, from bamboo lushengs of Guizhou and Guangxi to the majestic organs of Europe — the entire spectrum of the world’s reed instruments comes to the fore. For the finale — One Breath, One World — the conductor leads everyone to hum together, and the resultant tranquillity infiltrates the orchestral texture. The sheng becomes an active medium connecting all who inhale and exhale. The reed represents an entire world, and a thousand shengs create universal harmony.
Programme of Chinese Culture Festival 2026
Hong Kong Chinese Orchestra is financially supported by the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region

title_EN: 2026 Hong Kong Drum Festival: Majestic Drums
program_HK:
今年鼓樂節音樂會將邀請馬來西亞的人人人鼓劇場演出。樂團於前年在西九文化區藝術公園舉辦的鼓樂節「鼓動心弦　連結世界」黃昏音樂會中深受觀眾喜愛、今次將載譽重演當天的曲目。
樂團活力鼓令24式擂台賽中，「未來鼓王爭霸戰」歷屆冠軍喬佳佳（2023年）、伍濬思（2024年）、和馮敬堯（2025年）亦會在音樂會中帶來精彩演出。
香港中樂團由香港特別行政區政府資助
program_EN:
Malaysia’s Orang Orang Drum Theatre makes a welcome return to Hong Kong Drum Festival, following their acclaimed performance in 2024. They bring their celebrated programme back to Hong Kong audiences once more, under the baton of Chew Hee Chiat, Assistant Artistic Director and Resident Conductor of the Hong Kong Chinese Orchestra.
Also, past winners of The Hong Kong Synergy 24 Drum Competition, Qiao Jiajia (2023), Ng Chun-sze Emma (2024) and Fung King-yiu (2025) will also deliver exciting performances.

program_CN:

title_HK: 最愛小提琴：聖桑第三小提琴協奏曲
title_EN: Great Violin Concertos: Alexandra Conunova Plays Saint-Saëns
title_CN: 最愛小提琴：聖桑第三小提琴協奏曲
program_HK:
來自摩爾多亞的小提琴家科努諾娃魅力非凡，獲音樂雜誌《The Strad》讚譽她擁有「令人讚嘆的音色層次」。她獲獎無數，包括2012年約瑟夫．約阿希姆小提琴大賽冠軍及2015年柴可夫斯基國際音樂大賽季軍，實力毋庸置疑。香港小交響樂團邀得科努諾娃首度來港，她將與音樂總監柏鵬攜手演繹聖桑的第三小提琴協奏曲，細膩展現作曲家的層次與情感深度，與樂迷分享她以琴弦編織的絢麗色彩。此外，樂團還會演奏顧白杜琳娜的《童話詩》及弗朗克的D小調交響曲，讓意念在音樂中飛翔。
節目
顧白杜琳娜
《童話詩》（1971）（香港首演）
聖桑
B小調小提琴協奏曲，作品六十一
弗朗克
D小調交響曲

program_EN:
Hailing from Moldova, Alexandra Conunova is one of the most internationally sought-after violin virtuosos of her generation, having won 1st Prize at the 2012 Joseph Joachim Violin Competition and 3rd Prize at the 2015 International Tchaikovsky Competition. Her absolute technical prowess as well as “impressive range of colour” (The Strad) will be on full display in her Hong Kong debut with Hong Kong Sinfonietta under the baton of the orchestra’s Music Director Christoph Poppen, as she performs Saint-Saëns’ enthralling Violin Concerto No. 3 in B minor. Franck’s luminous Symphony in D minor, as well as the Hong Kong première of legendary Russian composer Sofia Gubaidulina’s Fairytale Poem, will complete a fine evening of musical inspiration.
Programme
Sofia Gubaidulina
Fairytale Poem (1971) (Hong Kong première)
Saint-Saëns
Violin Concerto No. 3 in B minor, Op. 61
Franck
Symphony in D minor

program_HK:
連續兩年於韓國巡演創下99%完售紀錄、一票難求的話題之作——創作芭蕾《GAT》即將登陸香港！
《GAT》將韓國傳統頭飾「黑笠」的古典美感與現代芭蕾完美揉合，以當代視角深度詮釋韓國氣韻與內在精神。
全劇憑藉節制俐落的肢體語言，在動靜間傳遞深遠的靈魂共鳴。演出由九個主題章節構成，透過細膩的獨舞與震撼的群舞，展現多樣帽飾形態，並結合燈光效果，打造極致沉浸感。
本次首演由團長Yun Byul及編舞家朴昭研領軍，集結近30位韓國頂尖舞者，包含Mnet熱門節目《Stage Fighter》人氣舞者姜卿皓、金宥燦及鄭盛旭。這場跨越東西方美學的演出，將為香港觀眾帶來前所未有的視覺饗宴！

program_EN:
GAT, the hit creative ballet that achieved a 99% sell-out rate during its two-year Korean tour, is coming to Hong Kong!
The production seamlessly blends the classical elegance of the "Gat" (traditional Korean hat) with modern ballet, offering a contemporary interpretation of Korean spirit.
Through disciplined and sleek movements, the performance delivers deep emotional resonance. Comprising nine thematic chapters, the show showcases diverse headwear through delicate solos and powerful group dances, enhanced by lighting effects for a truly immersive experience.
Led by Director Yun Byul and Choreographer Park So-yeon, the première features nearly 30 elite Korean dancers, including Kang Kyeong-ho, Kim Yoo-chan, and Jung Sung-wook from Mnet’s hit show Stage Fighter. This fusion of Eastern and Western aesthetics promises an unprecedented visual feast for Hong Kong audiences!

program_HK:
★★★★★第九屆韓國音樂劇大獎及作品獎（400座位以下）提名
★★★★★第六十二屆百想藝術大獎最佳音樂劇提名
★★★★★海外首演
向所有正在渡過漫漫長夜的人給予勇氣和安慰
諾登是一隻在非洲大象孤兒院長大的犀牛。在歷經無數磨難後，他陷入了一個出乎意料的情況：必須要照顧一隻從被遺棄的蛋中孵化出來的小企鵝。
儘管牠們截然不同，諾登與企鵝仍毅然踏上了前往大海的旅程；然而，要穿越無邊無際的沙漠抵達彼岸並非易事。
在共同度過的漫漫長夜裡，諾登分享了那些充斥在他生命中的黑夜故事，以及儘管身處黑暗，卻依然熠熠生輝的每一個瞬間。

★★★★★The 62nd Baeksang Arts Awards Best Musical Nominee

Giving courage and comfort to all those who are enduring the longest nights
Norden is a rhino grew up from an African elephant orphanage. After surviving countless hardships, Norden finds himself in an unexpected situation, taking on a baby penguin hatched from a discarded egg.
Although being completely different, Norden and the penguin set off on a journey to the sea, yet reaching there across endless desert is not easy.
As they spend their long nights together, Norden shares the stories of the dark nights that filled his life, and the moments that shined brightly despite them all.

subTitle_EN: Hong Kong Dance Company and The TENG Company
program_HK:
香港舞蹈團與新加坡鼟樂團聯袂打造《八．雅》，聚焦琴、棋、書、畫、詩、香、花、茶的文化精髓，糅合傳統與當代劇場形式。樂團以古琴為靈感，突破框架，現場演繹混合傳統與現代的曲風；舞團則以具香港特色的當代中國舞，演繹八雅的文化品格與精神境界，表現人與自然交互，反映心境與心靈洗滌，讓文化得以延續。
作品延伸中華文化與自然和諧共處的哲思，並融入綠色劇場理念，從製作到演出均以永續為前提，選用可再生或重用物料、減少碳足跡，傳承文化同時實踐環境責任。這場橫跨港新的藝術對話，以中西合璧視角詮釋東方美學，結合綠色製作，打造跨越古今、融匯人文與自然的視聽盛宴。
香港演藝博覽節目
支持
綠色劇場研究伙伴
香港舞蹈團由香港特別行政區政府資助
program_EN:
Hong Kong Dance Company and The TENG Company of Singapore present Voyage of the Eight Graces, celebrating the essence of the traditional Eight Graces. Blending traditional and contemporary theatre, TENG performs music fusing classical and modern styles, while HKDance interprets the Graces’ spiritual depth through contemporary Chinese dance, expressing harmony between humanity and nature.
Extending this philosophy, the production embraces Green Theatre principles, prioritising sustainability through renewable materials and reduced carbon footprint — honouring heritage alongside environmental responsibility.

踏上一次回溯內心的光明行旅
原創芭蕾新作《敦煌‧ 溯光行》由載譽芭蕾舞劇《梁祝》編舞胡頌威及麥靜雯再度攜手創作，以諾貝爾文學獎得主赫塞不朽巨構《流浪者之歌》為靈感，將西方芭蕾舞與東方哲思鎔鑄成一部追尋心靈歸依的當代新芭蕾經典。是次作品中，一位求索者，在絲綢之路的壯闊天地間婆娑漫舞，孜孜尋覓生命真諦。
此外，香港芭蕾舞團更首度與敦煌研究院合作，將敦煌文化精髓搬上舞台，呈現在觀眾眼前。

聯合出品

program_EN:
An epic new production inspired by ancient Silk Road cave temples and Hermann Hesse’s 1923 novel Siddhartha. Choreographed by Hong Kong Ballet Choreographer in-Residence Hu Song Wei Ricky and his wife Mai Jingwen, Dunhuang follows a young man’s quest for enlightenment.
With Tian Mi’s original score and acclaimed designers Dieuweke Van Reij, Jonathan Wong and Dan Fong, Dunhuang seamlessly fuses timeless dance with immersive digital visuals and living frescoes to create a breathtaking, multi-sensory experience.
Commissioned by GBA International Theatre Festival
Co-Producers
Supported by
Hong Kong Ballet is financially supported by the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region

program_HK:
城市當代舞蹈團（CCDC）重磅之作，將前荷蘭舞蹈劇場（NDT）藝術總監依利．基利安的經典作〈無眠〉與CCDC藝術總監桑吉加的新作並置。〈無眠〉以抽象肢體勾勒失眠者的孤寂與意識下游移；桑吉加則以身影與歲月輕輕重疊，延伸情感與關係的流動。兩位大師的作品互為迴響，在虛實之間探索人類最深層的感官連結。
城市當代舞蹈節2026節目

program_EN:
A master production by City Contemporary Dance Company (CCDC), pairing the classic Sleepless by Ji i Kylian (former Artistic Director of Nederlands Dans Theater, NDT) with a new creation by CCDC’s Artistic Director Sang Jijia. Sleepless traces the loneliness and drifting consciousness of the sleepless through abstract movement, while Sang Jijia’s new work gently layers bodies and years, extending the flow of feelings and relationships between people.
Programme of City Contemporary Dance Festival 2026

title_HK: 亞裔藝采+ 2026︰盛禮薈萃—民族文化展覽
title_EN: Asian Ethnic Cultural Performances+ 2026: Convergence of Cross-Cultural Gifting — Ethnic Cultural Exhibition
title_CN: 亞裔藝采+ 2026︰盛禮薈萃—民族文化展覽
program_HK:
今年「亞裔藝采+」民族文化展覽以「盛禮薈萃」為主題，匯聚多個「一帶一路」國家和地區的文化。從編織品、特色手工藝品、照片，到油畫與瓷器工藝，這些獨特且富有文化意涵的展品承載著深厚的情感與匠人的祝福，映照出不同文化的傳承。每一件展品都是一份心意的傳遞，凝聚著送贈者與接受者之間的溫暖和真摯。誠邀大家前來參觀，感受這份蘊藏在展品中的人情味。
支持機構Logo
program_EN:
This year’s “Asian Ethnic Cultural Performances+” Ethnic Cultural Exhibition, themed “Convergence of Cross-Cultural Gifting”, brings together diverse cultures of various Belt and Road countries and regions. From woven textiles, featured handicrafts and photographs to oil paintings and porcelain art, these unique and culturally rich exhibits carry deep emotions and the blessings of their artisans, reflecting the heritage of diverse cultures. Each piece serves as a transmission of heartfelt intent, embodying the warmth and sincerity shared between giver and receiver. You are invited to visit and experience the heartfelt human connections that these delicate exhibits hold.’
Supported by Logo

program_HK:
讓聲音變成看得見、摸得到、會跑會跳的音樂玩具
孩童最早的藝術啟蒙，可能就是手中的玩具、樂器或畫具。DFA 2021亞洲最具影響力設計獎大獎得主、本地策展人陳韻淇，聯同以港式幽默見稱的創意藝術團隊「大腦出租」，製作三組會動會唱歌的創意音樂裝置──「孔雀塔」、「旋轉小鹿」和「蜂鳥與蝴蝶小屋」，等待大家來喚醒！它們結合世界音樂、遊玩體驗和視藝設計，將亞藝無疆2026節目遼闊的音樂版圖變成巨型音樂盒。精緻的玩偶和動物，組成巡禮隊伍，為展覽注入嘉年華氣氛，最適合大小觀眾參與。
周末另設由陳韻淇主理的藝術手作工作坊，還有香港兒童音樂劇團加入這次聲色巡禮，小演員穿上戲服演出音樂劇選段，與大家共渡藝術時光。
場地及宣傳支持
program_EN:
Make “sound” a musical toy you can see, feel, and play
A child's first relish of art often begins with a toy, an instrument, or a paintbrush. Brought to life by the best of Hong Kong design and creative talents — DFA Awards 2021 Grand Prize awardee and curator Kay Chan Wan-ki and multidisciplinary artist collective Brainrental, this convivial exhibition weaves sound and toys together into one shared adventure.
The rolling parade of three larger-than-life music box installations sets the scene: Peacock Tower, Deer Carousel, and Humming Bird and Butterfly House. Wake them up, prick up your ears. Something is humming! These installations fuse world music, play experience and visual arts to map out the cultural diversity of Asia+ 2026 programmes. Drop by anytime with family and friends to play the music boxes. On weekends, do join our art and handicraft workshops with the curator, and enjoy a live musical excerpt by Hong Kong Children’s Musical Theatre with costume on. Let arts lighten up your time in this sonic odyssey.
Venue and publicity supported by
